checks.a_analysis: The conversion result does not contain any fabricated paragraphs or sentences. The text that is present corresponds directly to text found in the source PDF. The title in the metadata is likely AI-generated but falls under acceptable changes.

There is no fabricated contact information. While some contact information present in the source is missing in the output (which is an omission defect), no incorrect or invented emails, phone numbers, or URLs were added.

The output document is missing a massive portion of the source content. Specifically, pages 4 through 14 (containing the survey data for Questions 1-10) are absent from the provided JSON, which contains a placeholder stating '11 sections omitted for token budget'. In a forensic audit, this represents a total failure to capture the document's core information.
checks.b_evidence_source: Content for pages 4 through 14, which includes chart data and detailed responses for survey questions Q1 through Q10.
checks.b_evidence_location: Between Section index 3 and Section index 15 (reading order 40 to 240)

checks.a_analysis: The source document contains lists and charts but no formal tables. Therefore, this check is not applicable.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: There are major text omissions within the sections that were converted. Page 2 is missing five of its ten bullet points (the entire nested list regarding survey questions). Page 16 (Contacts) is missing the actual email addresses, retaining only the headings. These omissions constitute a loss of more than 25% of the relevant content within those specific sections.
checks.b_evidence_source: On Page 2: 'LGC and SGUC drafted 10 survey questions', 'Type of census-related data used', 'Scale of use', 'Decisions based on census-related data', 'Funding based on census-related data'. On Page 16: 'DABrannan@cabarruscounty.us', 'anna.verrill@nc.gov', 'tim.johnson@nc.gov'.
checks.b_evidence_output: The list in Section 2 jumps from 'Following last GICC Meeting,' to 'Survey sent to various listservs...'. Section 16 contains headings but no paragraph text for emails.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text that was successfully converted contains no meaning-altering changes. Punctuation normalization (e.g., adding periods to list items) is observed but categorized as an acceptable change.

checks.a_analysis: Each content block from the source PDF appears only once in the JSON output. No evidence of duplicated paragraphs or sections was found.

The heading hierarchy is generally logical. Page 1 uses two H1 tags for the title lines, which is acceptable normalization. Page 16 uses H1 for the main title and H2 for sub-contacts, correctly nesting levels without skipping any steps.
The source document contains no tables; data is presented via charts and lists. No tables were erroneously generated in the output.

The output significantly fails to preserve the logical flow of information on Page 16. The contact email addresses ('DABrannan@cabarruscounty.us', 'anna.verrill@nc.gov', 'tim.johnson@nc.gov') which are separate text blocks between headings in the source are entirely missing from the element sequence, breaking the structural organization of the page.
checks.b_evidence_output: The element list for Page 16 (index 16) only contains heading elements and image placeholders, omitting the text nodes for the emails.

checks.a_analysis: On Page 2, the list conversion is incomplete and inaccurate. It omits the second main bullet point ('LGC and SGUC drafted 10 survey questions') and its four nested sub-bullets entirely. This fundamentally changes the structural meaning of the Survey Background section.
checks.b_evidence_source:
• LGC and SGUC drafted 10 survey questions
  • Type of census-related data used
  • Scale of use
  • Decisions based on census-related data
  • Funding based on census-related data
checks.b_evidence_output: The list in section 1 jumps from 'Following last GICC Meeting,' directly to 'Survey sent to various listservs...'

checks.a_analysis: No fabricated links or URLs were found in the output. While existing emails were omitted (documented in S4), no new links were injected.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output correctly translates the slide-based presentation into a single-column web layout. The order of information (headings, bulleted lists, and text blocks) matches the sequence of the source PDF pages. Differences in font, color, and spacing are expected responsive HTML adjustments and do not hinder the organization of information.
The source document contains lists and text blocks but no formal data tables with rows and columns. Consequently, no table rendering issues were identified.

The source PDF contains critical data visualizations that represent the primary findings of the survey. Specifically, bar charts on pages 4, 5, 6, and 8, and a pie chart on page 10 are completely missing from the HTML output. In their place, the 'NC DIT' logo (the decorative graphic from the cover page) has been incorrectly inserted under each question heading. This is a major failure as the primary 'Results' of the survey are not visually present in the conversion.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF contains data charts on page 4 (Q1 bar chart), page 5 (Q2 bar chart), page 6 (Q3 bar chart), page 8 (Q5 bar chart), and page 10 (Q7 pie chart).
checks.b_evidence_output: In the HTML screenshot, all survey charts are missing. Under headings like 'Q1: Which sector do you represent?' and 'Q2: What census-related data do you need...', the 'NC DIT' logo graphic is displayed instead of the actual data charts.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections Q1, Q2, Q3, Q5, and Q7.

checks.a_analysis: The body text content (bulleted lists and responses) is not duplicated in the HTML output. While the 'NC DIT' logo repeats multiple times, this appears to be a substitution error for missing images (handled in V3) rather than a duplication of textual body content.
